prose:
Frontier-orbital gap of pyridine (HOMO–LUMO)
Method provenance
• Property: Koopmans-style HOMO–LUMO energy gap (electron-volts)
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` ≡ B3LYP/def2-TZVP with default density-fitting (PySCF)
• Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the user-supplied pyridine.xyz geometry (coordinates not re-optimized; DFT//input-xyz)
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet, RKS)
• Software: chemkit frontier-orbitals skill → PySCF backend (SCF converged in 10 cycles)

Result (gas-phase, B3LYP/def2-TZVP):
  – HOMO energy  = −7.2525992 eV
  – LUMO energy  = −1.112786 eV
  → HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.1398131 eV

SCF status & warnings
• SCF converged: true (10 cycles)
• Engine warnings: none reported (warnings array empty)

Reproducibility
The job was launched with:
  chemkit frontier-orbitals --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz
and wrote its JSON result to
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp3uqfpqe2/live.json
A live engine log was streamed to a .out file in the current working directory while the calculation ran (path emitted at launch).

No experimental comparison is provided; this is a purely computed DFT estimate.
